vaccines.name: Pfizer-BioNTech (COMIRNATY)
vaccines.heat_degradation_effect: Rapid mRNA single-strand hydrolysis and structural collapse of protective lipid nanoparticles, rendering the formula completely impotent.
vaccines.freeze_danger_effect: N/A (Designed for ultra-low freezing, but repeated freeze-thaw cycles after reconstitution destroy structural integrity.)
vaccines.handling_protocol: Must be used within 6 hours once altered or brought to room temperature. Diluted vials cannot be refrozen.

vaccines.name: Moderna (Spikevax)
vaccines.heat_degradation_effect: Thermal denaturation of genetic material fragments, causing a fast decline in active antibody-response stimulation capabilities.
vaccines.freeze_danger_effect: N/A (Safely stored in standard medical freezers.)
vaccines.handling_protocol: Quarantine the entire batch immediately if the BioFuse visual seal activates past the 8-hour marker.

vaccines.name: BCG Vaccine
vaccines.heat_degradation_effect: Accelerated death of live-attenuated bacterial structures. Severe drop in efficiency if exposed continuously to high heat for over 10 days.
vaccines.freeze_danger_effect: The freeze-dried powder can withstand freezing, but freezing the separate liquid diluent breaks the glass vial structure.
vaccines.handling_protocol: CRITICAL: Once the freeze-dried components are mixed with the liquid diluent, the vaccine must be thrown away within 6 hours regardless of temperature status.

vaccines.name: Rotarix / RotaTeq
vaccines.heat_degradation_effect: Gradual loss of live viral potency. Extended heat exposure prevents the vaccine from prompting proper intestinal immunity.
vaccines.freeze_danger_effect: Liquid structure is highly sensitive to accidental crystallization, changing the consistency permanently.
vaccines.handling_protocol: Store centrally in the refrigerator unit. Never place near the back cooling vents where temperatures slide below 32°F.

vaccines.name: MMR II
vaccines.heat_degradation_effect: Rapid breakdown of live viral components, making it ineffective against measles and rubella vectors.
vaccines.freeze_danger_effect: Safe to freeze as raw powder, but the liquid diluent used for mixing will crack if frozen.
vaccines.handling_protocol: Once pulled from storage and drawn into a syringe, it must be administered within 8 hours or destroyed.

vaccines.name: Engerix-B / Recombivax
vaccines.heat_degradation_effect: Highly heat resistant up to a month, but continuous extreme ambient exposure slowly unravels the surface antigen proteins.
vaccines.freeze_danger_effect: CRITICAL RISK - Freezing causes the aluminum adjuvant to form solid crystals. This totally destroys the vaccine's potency and causes severe tissue irritation if injected.
vaccines.handling_protocol: Strictly freeze-sensitive. If the temperature log drops below 32°F even once, perform a manual 'Shake Test' to check for crystallization spoilage.

vaccines.name: Infanrix / DTaP
vaccines.heat_degradation_effect: Slow denaturation of toxoid components, drastically reducing long-term immune memory creation.
vaccines.freeze_danger_effect: CRITICAL RISK - Permanent precipitation of the aluminum salt adjuvant. The uniform liquid breaks down into un-mixable white flakes.
vaccines.handling_protocol: Do not use dry ice or cold packs directly touching the box during local shipping logistics.
